message:
feat(runtime): Implement Python rich comparison protocol with subclass priority

When comparing two objects where one is a subclass of the other, Python
requires trying the subclass's method first. This commit implements that
behavior correctly.

Key changes:
- Add `bases` field to PyObjectVTable for inheritance chain tracking
- Add `isProperSubclassOf` to ObjectInstance for subclass detection
- Update PyValue.eql/lt/le/gt/ge to respect subclass priority
- Update operator.eq/le/etc to support cross-type comparison via PyValue
- Fix PyValue.from to wrap pointer-to-struct as .object (not .ptr)
- Generate __bases_vtables__ and __vtable__ in codegen for classes

Example behavior now correct:
  eq(B(), C()) where C(B) -> calls C.__eq__ first, then B.__eq__
  le(B(), C()) where C(B) -> calls C.__ge__ first, then B.__le__
